title: Convention (v2 stdlib derivation)
description: A human-codified organizational rule scoped to a slice of the org. Conventions are top-down authored intent — operators write them, version-control them, review them — distinct from procedural memory templates (which are bottom-up reinforced from observed runs). Each Convention names a body of guidance plus an enforcement_mode (advisory / warn / enforce) that the engine and downstream agents respect when the convention's scope matches. Derivation: compose(Policy[intent], Scope[applies_to]). Cross-references concept_ids (when the convention codifies a stabilized concept per Chomskian R18) and type_definitions (when it constrains how a type composes).

convention_body.description: The convention's content. summary is required free text; items is an optional structured checklist; references link to other governance primitives this convention codifies or constrains.

summary.description: The rule itself, written for humans. Markdown allowed. Keep short — the body of a convention is its essence; reference docs go elsewhere.

items.description: Optional ordered checklist or rule list. Each item is a short label suitable for a UI checkbox; the prose explanation of each item lives in summary.

enforcement_mode.description: How the convention is enforced. advisory: stored + visible but no runtime check. warn: violations log + surface in audit but don't block. enforce: violations block the action.

convention_status.description: Lifecycle state. active: visible + enforced per enforcement_mode. archived: historical record, no enforcement; preserved for audit + concept-promotion provenance.

convention_reference.description: Optional cross-reference to a concept or type-definition this convention codifies. v064 ships the field shape; cross-validation against existing concept_id / type_definition_id rows happens at apply time on the engine.

id.description: UUID for concept / type_definition kinds. Null for stdlib kind references where name carries the identity.

applies_to_scope_ref.description: Primary scope this convention governs. Resources within this scope (or its descendants per the scope tree) check the convention when relevant actions fire.

additional_scope_refs.description: Rare — additional scopes the convention also governs. Most conventions need only the primary applies_to_scope_ref; populate this when one rule legitimately spans multiple scope subtrees.

references.description: Optional cross-references to concepts (Chomskian R18 — convention codifies a stabilized regularity), type_definitions (convention constrains a type), or stdlib kinds.
